key_insight: Ga3+ and In3+ substitute different Fe sites in BaFe12O19, which switches the magnetic state and fundamentally changes the dielectric and magnetodielectric mechanisms across temperature.
engineering_relevance: Site-selective cation doping offers a practical route to tune ferrite behavior for temperature-dependent magnetic, dielectric, and multifunctional device applications.
method: Prepared BaFe12-xMexO19 (M = Ga, In) ceramics by solid-state reaction.
Compared magnetic, dielectric, and magnetodielectric responses from 10 K to high temperature.
Assigned mechanisms using site preference, spin ordering, electron hopping, dipole glass, and Maxwell-Wagner effects.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports distinct temperature-dependent trends and mechanism assignments across pure, Ga-doped, and In-doped samples, including ferrimagnetic, non-collinear-to-collinear transitions, and different MD sign changes.
watch_item: Mechanism interpretation is primarily correlational from temperature-dependent property trends, so device design should verify site occupancy and transport/magnetic coupling under operating conditions.
paper_title: Different dielectric, magnetic, and magnetodielectric mechanisms in M-type BaFe12O19 hexaferrite regulated by doping Ga3+ and In3+ cations
